sketch0.title: Why Date Functions Matter in SQL

fb1.title: Identifying a Date Function
fb1.prompt: Which of the following is a built-in SQL function for working with dates?

help.concept: Date functions are used to manipulate or extract information from date values. Aggregate functions like SUM, COUNT, and AVG are for numeric calculations.
help.hint_1: Look for a function that works with date or time values.
help.hint_2: the missing term is used to format or extract parts of dates in SQL.

mc1.title: When to Use Date Functions
mc1.prompt: In which scenarios would you use a date function in SQL? (Choose all that apply)
mc1.hint: Think about which tasks involve dates or periods.

help.concept: Date functions are used when you need to manipulate or filter data based on dates or time periods.
help.hint_1: Consider which scenarios involve extracting or filtering by date or term.
help.hint_2: Extracting parts of a date and filtering by semester both use date or string functions.

sk1.title: Why Date Functions Matter in SQL
sk1.bodyMarkdown: Date functions in SQL help you extract, format, or calculate with date and time values. Even if your table stores dates as text (like 'Spring 2026'), SQL provides functions to manipulate and compare these values. This is especially useful for filtering records by term, calculating durations, or grouping by year or month.

sk2.title: Common Date Functions
sk2.bodyMarkdown: Some common SQL date functions include `DATE()`, `STRFTIME()`, and `CURRENT_DATE`. These can convert text to date, extract parts of a date, or get the current date. For example, `STRFTIME('%Y', term)` would extract the year from a term column if it were in a standard date format.

sk3.title: Date Functions and School Data
sk3.bodyMarkdown: In the school_relations_intro dataset, the `enrollments` table has a `term` column with values like 'Spring 2026'. While this is not a standard date format, you can still use string functions or date functions to filter or group by term. Understanding how to work with these values is key for reporting by academic term or year.
